title: The Cold War

jokes.text: Way to go, Arnold Schwarzenegro.
jokes.setup: Carlton brags about pressing 180 pounds
jokes.punchline: Will creates a portmanteau combining Arnold Schwarzenegger with 'Negro' to mock Carlton's small frame
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures Will's quick wit and cultural references, while highlighting the physical contrast between Carlton and actual bodybuilders

jokes.text: Yeah, I know. I don't see what attracted her to you.
jokes.setup: Carlton describes Paula as beautiful and intelligent with a great body
jokes.punchline: Will agrees but turns it into an insult about Carlton's attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection joke with excellent economy - leads Carlton to think Will agrees, then delivers the burn. Classic sitcom structure executed well

jokes.text: Maybe you should go to bed.
jokes.setup: Carlton lists romantic reminders everywhere - grass, beach blanket, swing
jokes.punchline: Will suggests the most obvious romantic location Carlton hasn't mentioned
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - Will's practical response to Carlton's romantic melodrama lands as understated humor

jokes.text: Oh, good, a floor show.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil wants to resolve the baby gender debate 'right here, right now'
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey sarcastically treats it like entertainment
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's dry British wit commenting on family drama - fits his character perfectly as the observant butler

jokes.text: If it's a boy, it's blue.
jokes.setup: Ashley asks how you tell if a baby is a boy or girl from sonogram
jokes.punchline: Hilary thinks it's literally colored blue or pink like baby clothes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - her superficial understanding of the world leads to genuinely silly logic that's completely in character

jokes.text: Hilary's not planning on having kids, is she?
jokes.setup: Hilary's confused explanation about sonogram colors
jokes.punchline: Ashley's innocent concern about Hilary reproducing
jokes.explanation: Ashley's innocent delivery makes this burn even funnier - the youngest Banks child recognizing Hilary shouldn't pass on her genes

jokes.text: Now this is exactly why the doctor changed his number on us, Vivian.
jokes.setup: Vivian keeps flip-flopping on whether she wants to know the baby's gender
jokes.punchline: Phil reveals their indecisiveness has driven their doctor to avoid them
jokes.explanation: Great escalation joke that shows the real-world consequences of their indecision - earned humor from character behavior

jokes.text: See, this is the same dude that said a squirrel ate that six-pack of Milky Ways.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil promises not to look at the sonogram
jokes.punchline: Will references Phil's past lie about eating candy
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that reinforces Uncle Phil's weakness for food while questioning his trustworthiness - shows Will knows his uncle well

jokes.text: I got dressed all by myself this morning.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asks if Geoffrey can handle holding the sonogram
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey sarcastically demonstrates his competence with basic tasks
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's dry sarcasm about being treated like he's incompetent - perfectly fits his character as the long-suffering butler

jokes.text: Now, this friend of mine knows this squaw that he's completely in love with, right? But this particular squaw just broke up with his best friend.
jokes.setup: Will wants advice about dating Carlton's ex
jokes.punchline: Will awkwardly uses Native American metaphors to describe modern dating
jokes.explanation: Will's attempt to disguise his question with inappropriate cultural metaphors - awkward and dated humor that doesn't land well

jokes.text: Word up.
jokes.setup: Will says 'Really?' about Uncle Phil's legal case
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil uses Will's street slang awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Uncle Phil trying to be cool with Will's slang is inherently funny due to the character contrast - simple but effective

jokes.text: No. Let's just say he's two olives short of a martini.
jokes.setup: Will asks if the guy in the story died from being shot
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil uses a colorful euphemism for brain damage
jokes.explanation: Creative euphemism that's both funny and fits Uncle Phil's more sophisticated vocabulary - memorable phrasing

jokes.text: I'll rock your world.
jokes.setup: Will calls Paula to confirm their date
jokes.punchline: Will's overconfident pickup line
jokes.explanation: Generic pickup line that shows Will's cockiness but lacks originality - serviceable character moment

jokes.text: And I'd feel a lot better if you'd wash your own underthings.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil says he'd feel better if Geoffrey called him Phil
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey counters with his own preference about laundry duties
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's dry response deflates Uncle Phil's attempt at friendliness by bringing up unglamorous butler duties

jokes.text: Like one hell of a night in Tijuana.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil offers Geoffrey $100 to show the sonogram
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey compares it to buying prostitutes in Mexico
jokes.explanation: Unexpected dirty humor from the usually proper Geoffrey - subverts his character in a surprising way

jokes.text: At least she offered me $200.
jokes.setup: Vivian scolds Uncle Phil for bribing Geoffrey with $100
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey reveals Vivian offered him twice as much
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that reveals Vivian's hypocrisy while showing Geoffrey enjoys stirring up trouble - excellent structure

jokes.text: Maybe I should just find out.
jokes.setup: Will pretends not to know why Paula would call him
jokes.punchline: Will's obviously fake confusion about Paula's interest
jokes.explanation: Will's transparent attempt to act innocent - basic cringe comedy but not particularly clever

jokes.text: We might have to pull an all nighter.
jokes.setup: Will pretends he's talking about homework with Paula
jokes.punchline: Double entendre about studying vs. sexual activity
jokes.explanation: Standard double entendre - serviceable but predictable humor about teenage sexual desire

jokes.text: Maybe you're on to something.
jokes.setup: Ashley warns Will that Carlton might never speak to him again
jokes.punchline: Will considers this a positive outcome
jokes.explanation: Will's callousness toward Carlton shows his selfish side - dark but fits the character conflict

jokes.text: Paula. You're right. I hope that doesn't happen tonight.
jokes.setup: Will confuses Paula and Denise's names
jokes.punchline: Will 'corrects' himself but makes a sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Weak sexual innuendo that relies on cheap humor - the name confusion adds nothing meaningful

jokes.text: No, I don't know nothing. I'm just a poor black man trying to make it in Bel-Air.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks if Will knows what Paula did
jokes.punchline: Will plays up his outsider status to avoid answering
jokes.explanation: Will references the show's premise while deflecting - good character work that acknowledges the class dynamic

jokes.text: Most girls don't give you nothing.
jokes.setup: Carlton complains Paula gave him the flu
jokes.punchline: Will suggests Carlton should be grateful for any female attention
jokes.explanation: Will's burn about Carlton's dating life - economical delivery that reinforces their relationship dynamic

jokes.text: Get out of the house.
jokes.setup: Ashley says she doesn't feel well
jokes.punchline: Vivian immediately banishes her sick daughter
jokes.explanation: Vivian's immediate, harsh reaction to protect her pregnancy - the abrupt shift from caring mom to self-preservation is funny

jokes.text: You know, we're getting more and more like the Jackson family every day.
jokes.setup: Family members are being kicked out of the house
jokes.punchline: Will compares them to the dysfunctional Jackson family
jokes.explanation: Topical reference to the Jackson family drama of the early 90s - works for the time period but may not age well

jokes.text: You want to start paying rent? I can be out in an hour.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil threatens Will with paying rent
jokes.punchline: Will immediately agrees to move out rather than pay
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Will's street-smart practicality and refusal to be taken advantage of

jokes.text: Yes, Ashley, it means she's having a better week.
jokes.setup: Ashley asks if Paula being Carlton's girlfriend means anything
jokes.punchline: Will focuses on Paula's upgrade in dating prospects
jokes.explanation: Will's arrogant assessment that he's better than Carlton - economical and character-appropriate

jokes.text: I mean, come on, he thinks X is the movie about Malcolm-Jamal Warner's fight for freedom.
jokes.setup: Will says Ashley gives Carlton too much credit for intelligence
jokes.punchline: Will claims Carlton confuses Malcolm X with Malcolm from The Cosby Show
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture joke that plays on Carlton's supposed naivety about Black culture - good cultural reference mixing

jokes.text: Very funny, guys. Why don't you stop spinning?
jokes.setup: Carlton is sick and disoriented
jokes.punchline: Carlton thinks the room is spinning when he's just dizzy
jokes.explanation: Standard sick person comedy - Carlton's confusion about his symptoms is mildly amusing but predictable

jokes.text: That'd feel kind of good right now.
jokes.setup: Will references a Star Trek episode about brain-boring insects
jokes.punchline: Carlton says that would feel better than his current state
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about preferring death to illness - Carlton's dramatic response fits his character's tendency to overreact

jokes.text: Yeah, so was I. No, see, you don't know about it. See, you ain't hip to it, right? In Philly, we name our food, right? See, like cheesecake is Paula, right? And a Snack Pack is Boomshika.
jokes.setup: Will accidentally admits to dating Paula, then tries to cover
jokes.punchline: Will creates increasingly ridiculous explanation about naming food
jokes.explanation: Will's desperate improvisation creates absurd comedy - 'Boomshika' as a Snack Pack name is memorably ridiculous

jokes.text: I can't see him. You can. Is he buying it?
jokes.setup: Will is presumably hiding behind something
jokes.punchline: Will asks the audience if Carlton believes his lie
jokes.explanation: Fourth wall break that acknowledges the audience - fits the show's style of Will occasionally addressing viewers

jokes.text: I did it to save you from something terrible. As soon as I figure it out, I'll let you know what it is.
jokes.setup: Will claims he dated Paula to save Carlton
jokes.punchline: Will admits he hasn't figured out what he's saving Carlton from
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Will's desperate excuses - the honesty about not having a plan is funnier than any excuse he could make

jokes.text: Carlton, who are you calling? Your mother.
jokes.setup: Carlton threatens to call Will's mother
jokes.punchline: Will realizes Carlton is actually going through with the nuclear option
jokes.explanation: Escalation to the ultimate sibling warfare - calling the parent. Will's horror is genuine and relatable

jokes.text: So it's okay to steal a guy's girlfriend as long as you don't go tell his mommy about it.
jokes.setup: Will says calling his mother crosses the line
jokes.punchline: Carlton points out the hypocrisy in Will's moral code
jokes.explanation: Carlton's logical response highlighting Will's twisted priorities - good character moment showing Carlton's intelligence

jokes.text: Even in ancient times, they would kill your cattle, they would burn your village, and they would stone your family, but they wouldn't never tell your mother, man.
jokes.setup: Will explains why calling his mother is worse than girlfriend theft
jokes.punchline: Will creates absurd historical precedent for respecting maternal boundaries
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that creates a ridiculous moral hierarchy - the idea that ancient warriors had better ethics about tattling is absurdly funny

jokes.text: It was Halloween. You still haven't returned my bustier.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil mentions Carlton dressing in his clothes
jokes.punchline: Carlton borrowed Uncle Phil's women's undergarments for Halloween
jokes.explanation: Reveals Uncle Phil owns women's lingerie and Carlton borrowed it - unexpected character detail that's both disturbing and funny

jokes.text: Look like you spending the evening with Little Richard.
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions spending evening with radio and Madonna
jokes.punchline: Will implies Carlton's radio reception is poor so Madonna sounds like Little Richard
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about poor radio quality changing high-pitched Madonna to sound like Little Richard's falsetto

jokes.text: She has parents? She never introduced me to her parents. She told me they were dead.
jokes.setup: Will mentions Paula's parents wouldn't let him stay over
jokes.punchline: Carlton reveals Paula lied about her parents being dead
jokes.explanation: Reveals Paula's deception and Carlton's naivety - great escalation that shows how badly she treated Carlton

jokes.text: Man, nice, firm mattress, just like the one at Paula's house.
jokes.setup: Will comments on Carlton's bed quality
jokes.punchline: Will implies intimate knowledge of Paula's bedroom
jokes.explanation: Will's tactless bragging about sexual conquest - creates cringe but lacks creativity

jokes.text: and over, and over...
jokes.setup: Will says Paula thanked him 'over and over'
jokes.punchline: Will keeps repeating 'over' with sexual implication
jokes.explanation: Juvenile sexual bragging that's more annoying than funny - relies on repetition rather than wit

jokes.text: Why don't you look like one?
jokes.setup: Will tells Carlton to act like an adult
jokes.punchline: Carlton says Will doesn't look like an adult
jokes.explanation: Simple comeback with perfect economy - Carlton gets a rare win over Will with this basic but effective retort

jokes.text: Whatever you say. [Throws remote hard] Go get it. Make me.
jokes.setup: Will asks Carlton to toss the remote
jokes.punchline: Carlton throws it hard, starting a physical confrontation
jokes.explanation: Physical escalation of their conflict - Carlton's rare moment of assertiveness leads to slapstick violence

jokes.text: Make me.
jokes.setup: Will telling Carlton to go get the remote
jokes.punchline: Carlton's childish challenge
jokes.explanation: Playground-level antagonism - sets up physical confrontation but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: Philip, honey, at least take off the foil.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil finds the chocolate Easter bunny he's been looking for
jokes.punchline: Vivian tells him to unwrap it before eating
jokes.explanation: Shows Uncle Phil's eagerness to eat candy and Vivian's practical concern - reinforces Phil's food obsession

jokes.text: Hi, this is Hilary. Roses are red, violets are blue, I'm not in so... so leave a...
jokes.setup: Hilary's answering machine message attempts poetry
jokes.punchline: Hilary can't finish the rhyme scheme properly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - attempts to be poetic but lacks the intelligence to complete it, showing her superficial nature

jokes.text: First, I'd nibble on your left ear, then I'd bite you on your neck. Then I'd... Oops. There's my other line.
jokes.setup: Paula leaves a sexy message for her 'poor little baby'
jokes.punchline: Paula gets interrupted mid-seduction by another call
jokes.explanation: Paula's sexy message gets interrupted, showing she's juggling multiple guys - reinforces her manipulative character

jokes.text: Yeah, what are you going to do? Jump up and bang your head against it?
jokes.setup: Carlton threatens to beat Will up
jokes.punchline: Will mocks Carlton's short stature and limited fighting ability
jokes.explanation: Will's height joke about Carlton is visual and character-specific - good physical comedy reference

jokes.text: You're gonna go play on the freeway?
jokes.setup: Will says he's going to take Uncle Phil's advice
jokes.punchline: Carlton suggests the advice was suicide
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that's a bit too casual about suicide - Carlton's anger leads to harsh suggestion

jokes.text: I told her you had tuberculosis.
jokes.setup: Will promises to be honest about what happened with Paula
jokes.punchline: Will admits to lying about Carlton having tuberculosis
jokes.explanation: Will's confession reveals his sabotage tactics - tuberculosis is a good choice for a disease that would scare off a girlfriend

jokes.text: You told her about Binky? I'll kill you.
jokes.setup: Will mentions Carlton's hand puppet
jokes.punchline: Carlton is more upset about the puppet revelation than previous confessions
jokes.explanation: Carlton having a hand puppet named Binky is perfect character detail - his priorities about what's embarrassing are revealing

jokes.text: Well, I told her you worship Satan.
jokes.setup: Carlton explains why Letisha stopped calling Will
jokes.punchline: Carlton's sabotage involved claiming Will is a Satanist
jokes.explanation: Carlton's escalation to Satan worship shows his own capacity for sabotage - darker than expected from his character

jokes.text: Well, I told her you worship Satan.
jokes.setup: Carlton getting revenge with his own confession
jokes.punchline: Extreme accusation about Will's religious practices
jokes.explanation: Escalation of the confession game with absurdly serious accusation from Carlton

jokes.text: Well, I told her you shave your legs.
jokes.setup: Carlton explains Ashley's strange behavior toward Will
jokes.punchline: Carlton told Ashley that Will shaves his legs
jokes.explanation: Carlton's lie about leg shaving is emasculating and explains Ashley's weird behavior - good escalation in their confession war

jokes.text: You know that funky smell coming from the basement? Psych.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks about Jazz and mentions a basement smell
jokes.punchline: Carlton reveals he's joking about presumably killing Jazz
jokes.explanation: Carlton's fake-out about murdering Jazz - good misdirection that subverts the dark implication with 'Psych'

jokes.text: this stuff kind of tastes like dry wall with raisins in it.
jokes.setup: Aunt Viv serves what she calls great oatmeal
jokes.punchline: Will honestly describes the terrible taste
jokes.explanation: Will's honest food review with vivid imagery - drywall comparison is specific and funny

jokes.text: you really shouldn't wear silk no more 'cause, like, when big folks wear silk...
jokes.setup: Will continues his honesty streak
jokes.punchline: Will tactlessly comments on Uncle Phil's weight and fabric choices
jokes.explanation: Will's tactless fashion advice about Uncle Phil's size - mean-spirited honesty that's more cruel than funny

jokes.text: I'm bloody sick of the two of you going through my drawers.
jokes.setup: Everyone is being brutally honest
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey reveals his own grievance about privacy invasion
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's rare moment of anger about his privacy being violated - good character moment showing his frustration

jokes.text: You mean this isn't oatmeal?
jokes.setup: Vivian says 'In keeping with all this honesty'
jokes.punchline: Will realizes the oatmeal revelation means it's something else entirely
jokes.explanation: Will's realization that the bad food wasn't even oatmeal - simple callback that lands mildly

jokes.text: Congratulations, you're having a Hilary.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil reveals the 'sonogram' is actually a $25,000 bill
jokes.punchline: Phil compares the expensive bill to their expensive daughter Hilary
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline that ties together the episode's medical bill subplot with Hilary's established character trait of being expensive - excellent structure and character integration

jokes.text: It's the little chocolate Easter bunny!
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil keeps finding and losing the chocolate bunny
jokes.punchline: Phil's repeated excitement about the same candy
jokes.explanation: Running gag about Uncle Phil's food obsession - repetitive but fits his character's relationship with food

tmdb_overview: When Vivian quarantines Will and Carlton in the backyard pool house after both are stricken with contagious colds, they spend the night arguing over a girl that Will stole from Carlton.
